assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each have 'Verdict: PASS' covering single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively. report.md lines 50-54 detail all 5 criteria with specific code-level evidence.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: `modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs`, `modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs` (Files to Modify) and creates `tests/api/package.rs` (Files to Create)'. Detailed analysis in lines 34-36 confirms 'No out-of-scope files are present and no task-specified files are missing.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. Line 26: 'No review feedback to process. No sub-tasks created.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). No code files were created or modified. The report references code via quoted snippets and analysis but does not indicate any file modifications were made. All output is verification/reporting content.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents detailed findings but contains no merge commands, no gh pr merge invocations, and no indication of auto-merging. The report's structure (summary table + detailed domain analysis) is designed for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files'. Detailed analysis in lines 42-43: 'All added lines were scanned for hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud provider credentials, and database credentials. No matches found.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` created with 4 test functions; no existing test files modified or deleted'. Detailed analysis in line 80: 'The only test file (`tests/api/package.rs`) is newly created (not present in the base branch). All 4 test functions are additions. No existing test files were modified or deleted. Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected rows: Scope Containment (line 7), Diff Size (line 8), Commit Traceability (line 9), Sensitive Patterns (line 10), CI Status (line 11), Acceptance Criteria (line 12), Test Quality (line 13, explicitly combining: 'no repetitive test patterns detected... Eval Quality: N/A'), Test Change Classification (line 14), Verification Commands (line 15). The detailed analysis section organizes these under four domain headings: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 34-39), 'Security' (lines 42-43), 'Correctness' (lines 46-56), 'Style/Conventions' (lines 60-80). Test Quality combines Repetitive Test Detection (lines 62-68), Test Documentation (lines 70-76), and Eval Quality (line 78).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Detailed Domain Analysis' section (lines 30-80): Intent Alignment (lines 34-39) lists specific files and their task-specified categories (Files to Modify vs Files to Create), approximate line counts per file. Security (lines 42-43) describes specific pattern categories scanned (hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud provider credentials, database credentials) and identifies safe patterns (spdx::Expression import, AppError::BadRequest). Correctness (lines 48-56) provides per-criterion code-level verification with function names, SQL behavior, and test mapping for all 5 criteria. Style/Conventions (lines 60-80) includes per-test-function analysis of distinct behaviors, setup requirements, and doc comment quotations. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive code-level evidence per acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive test patterns detected... Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 78: 'Eval Quality: No eval result reviews detected on this PR. Verdict: N/A.' The Test Quality verdict is PASS based only on Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS), with Eval Quality explicitly N/A and not affecting the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 78: 'Eval Quality: No eval result reviews detected on this PR. Verdict: N/A.' Line 26: 'No sub-tasks created.' No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. Since Eval Quality is N/A (not WARN), no eval failure sub-tasks were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear in the summary table independently from the four domain sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions). The detailed domain analysis (lines 30-80) does not include Review Feedback or Root-Cause Investigation — they are only in the orchestrator-level summary table. Line 60 mentions 'No review comments classified as suggestions exist on this PR' under Style/Conventions Convention Upgrade (a separate check), confirming the orchestrator independently determined the Review Feedback verdict.
